title: Neubeckia humilis
taxonomy: Neubeckia humilis is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteliaceae. It is a species of the genus Neubeckia, which is native to New Zealand. The plant is a member of the order Asterales, which includes daisies and sunflowers. Neubeckia humilis is further classified as a perennial plant with small white flowers. This classification allows for the identification and understanding of the plant's characteristics and behaviors.
uses: Neubeckia humilis is also known as New Zealand flax, and it has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In some cultures, Neubeckia humilis is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat wounds and injuries. The plant's fibre has also been used to make a treatment for burns. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Neubeckia humilis for medicinal purposes.

intro: Neubeckia humilis, also known as New Zealand tea tree or New Zealand broom, is a shrub-like plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and a woody stem. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Neubeckia humilis contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Neubeckia humilis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to alleviate skin irritations and wounds.
